content:
- `task_switch.sh` completed and advanced Ralph state. The current task pointer file is gone afterward, which appears to be the intended handoff behavior in this repo.
- Final status review shows the expected task/report/plan artifacts plus Ralph bookkeeping changes; now enumerating the untracked files before staging and committing all required changes.
- Next: `git add -A`, commit with the required task-finished message including evidence from the full gate run, push, and stop.

- Enumerated the untracked plan/report/evidence files and confirmed they belong to this completed task, along with the expected Ralph bookkeeping updates.
- Staging and committing everything now with the required `task finished ...` subject and a body that records the manual-trial scope plus the passing `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long` evidence.
- Next: push the commit and stop.

- Verified the post-push dirty state was just the regenerated timestamps in `.ralph/current_tasks.md` and `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`.
- Committing that tiny Ralph bookkeeping follow-up now so the branch ends clean.
- Next: push the final cleanup commit, confirm `git status` is clean, and stop.

- Read prior Ralph progress log to recover the last handoff state.
- Inspected the active-task and done-task summaries plus the list-active-tasks skill instructions.
- Current visible state shows one active failing task in story-14 reports; next I am refreshing Ralph summaries and confirming the actual stack from repo manifests before selecting the task.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries and re-read the choose-task rules.
- Confirmed the actual project stack from manifests: Rust 2024 workspace with crates for runner, ingest-contract, and source-bootstrap; build gates are cargo clippy and cargo test via Makefile.
- Next I am checking the refreshed summaries for blocker and priority tags, then I will write the highest-precedence task path to .ralph/current_task.txt and stop.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries and re-read the choose-task rules before selection.
- Confirmed the actual repo is a Rust 2024 workspace (`Cargo.toml`, `Makefile`, `README.md`) with crates `runner`, `ingest-contract`, and `source-bootstrap`; gates are `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` and `cargo test --workspace`.
- Active backlog contains exactly one visible unblocked failing task and no active failing bugs: `.ralph/tasks/story-14-reports/02-task-report-code-complexity-and-kiss-assessment.md`.
- Wrote that relative path into `.ralph/current_task.txt` per choose-task precedence.
- Next, quit immediately so the execution agent can take this selected report task and complete it, then rerun `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint`.

- Read prior Ralph progress and confirmed the selected task path is `.ralph/tasks/story-14-reports/02-task-report-code-complexity-and-kiss-assessment.md`.
- Loaded the required `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions for this turn.
- Next I am reading the task file itself to determine whether I must create/adjust a plan or execute an existing `NOW EXECUTE` plan.

- Read the current task file. It is still a raw `not_started` task with no `TO BE VERIFIED` or `NOW EXECUTE` marker.
- Per task instructions, this turn must create a plan under `.ralph/tasks/story-14-reports/02-task-report-code-complexity-and-kiss-assessment_plans/` and then stop.
- Next I am inspecting existing Ralph plan conventions and the current repo layout so the plan is executable and aligned with `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries`.

- Confirmed other Ralph tasks record the selected plan path inside the task markdown using a `<plan>...</plan>` tag.
- Inspected the existing novice-report plan to mirror its level of detail and execution-stop conventions.
- Next I am reading the current task markdown convention sample plus the repo entrypoints so the new code-complexity report plan targets real module boundaries.

- Read the README plus runner and source-bootstrap entrypoints to ground the report plan in real public interfaces and crate/module boundaries.
- Confirmed the report must analyze a Rust workspace centered on `runner`, `source-bootstrap`, and `ingest-contract`, with `runner` owning most runtime complexity.
- Next I am reading a compact source tree and manifest metadata so the plan can specify concrete analysis slices and artifact structure.

- Enumerated workspace manifests and confirmed the real code surface is compact: one tiny shared contract crate, one source bootstrap crate, and one larger runner crate with nested runtime modules.
- The plan can therefore name concrete analysis targets rather than generic categories.
- Next I am reading the remaining runner module roots to map the main boundaries for the report slices.

- Created the plan artifact `.ralph/tasks/story-14-reports/02-task-report-code-complexity-and-kiss-assessment_plans/2026-04-19-code-complexity-kiss-report-plan.md` for the code-complexity/KISS report task.
- Updated `.ralph/tasks/story-14-reports/02-task-report-code-complexity-and-kiss-assessment.md` to include the `<plan>` link.
- After checking the plan against local Ralph conventions and the real workspace/module layout, flipped the plan terminator from `TO BE VERIFIED` to `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next turn should execute the plan as written: build the report artifacts under `.ralph/tasks/story-14-reports/artifacts/report-code-complexity/`, use vertical-slice evidence gathering, apply the improve-code-boundaries smell pass, then run `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long` before marking passes.

- Read prior Ralph progress, current task pointer, and the required `improve-code-boundaries` and `tdd` skill files.
- Confirmed the selected task is `.ralph/tasks/story-14-reports/02-task-report-code-complexity-and-kiss-assessment.md` and the previous handoff says the linked plan is already `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next I am reading the task markdown and its linked plan so I can execute the report work exactly as written.

- Read the task markdown and linked plan file.
- Confirmed the plan terminator is `NOW EXECUTE`, so this turn must perform the report work and complete the full validation sequence if successful.
- Next I am mapping the workspace manifests, crate entrypoints, and real module tree so I can draft the artifacts in vertical slices with concrete evidence.

- Created the required report artifact files under `.ralph/tasks/story-14-reports/artifacts/report-code-complexity/`.
- Wrote the first slice into `summary.md` and `module-inventory.md`, covering workspace composition, crate responsibilities, and initial boundary assessments.
- Next I am tracing the actual runtime interaction flows and hotspot files, then I will cross-check the test surface for structural smells and fold those findings into the remaining artifacts.

- Finished the interaction-flow, hotspot, and improve-code-boundaries smell pass for the report.
- Expanded all five report artifacts with evidence-backed findings, including concrete KISS recommendations and explicit notes on duplicated mapping shapes, oversized modules, test-harness duplication, and internal panic/expect boundaries.
- Next I am running the required validation sequence: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long`, then I will mark the task as passed and perform the Ralph task-switch/commit/push flow if everything stays green.

- Validation completed successfully: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long` all passed.
- The long lane took several minutes and emitted expected Cockroach/Postgres setup noise, but all ignored tests ultimately finished green.
- Next I am updating the task markdown for completion, then I will run `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, commit all changes with the required message, push, and stop.

- Updated the task markdown to `<status>completed</status>` and `<passes>true</passes>`, and checked off the acceptance criteria to match the completed report artifacts and passing gate results.
- No AGENTS.md update is needed; there were no durable repo-level surprises that would help future engineers more than the existing instructions.
- Next I am running `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, then I will stage all changes, commit with the required task-finished message including validation evidence, push, and stop.
